title: Thelypteris hirtopilosa
taxonomy: Thelypteris hirtopilosa is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Thelypteris hirtopilosa is classified as a part of the genus Thelypteris and belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The classification of Thelypteris hirtopilosa at the kingdom level is Plantae, and it is further categorized into the subkingdom Viridiplantae. Thelypteris hirtopilosa is also classified under the phylum Marchantiophyta, however, some sources place it under the phylum Psilotophyta.

intro: Thelypteris hirtopilosa is a species of fern, specifically a type of temperate deciduous fern, characterized by its hairy or hirsute rachis and pinnae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris hirtopilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Thelypteris hirtopilosa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues.
